edges.description: Loss of ATGL co-activation prevents triacylglycerol hydrolysis, so TG droplets accumulate.
In parallel (ATGL-independently), loss of the ABHD5 epidermal lipid role impairs the skin barrier.

evidence.snippet: Mutations in ABHD5/CGI58 gene are associated with CDS

nodes.description: Biallelic ABHD5 (CGI-58) variants eliminate the lipid-droplet co-activator of adipose triglyceride lipase (ATGL); ABHD5 also has an ATGL-independent role in epidermal lipid metabolism underlying the ichthyosis.

evidence.reference_title: Neutral Lipid Storage Diseases: clinical/genetic features and natural history in a large cohort of Italian patients.
evidence.snippet: Neutral Lipid Storage Diseases (NLSDs: NLSD type M with Myopathy and NLSD type I with Ichthyosis)

nodes.description: ABHD5/CGI-58 has an ATGL-independent role in epidermal lipid metabolism (acylceramide synthesis via PNPLA1); its loss impairs the skin permeability barrier, underlying the ichthyosis that defines NLSD type I.

evidence.snippet:
severe skin permeability barrier defect
uncovers an essential ATGL-independent role of CGI-58 in skin lipid metabolism

nodes.description: Undegraded triacylglycerol accumulates as cytoplasmic lipid droplets in skin, liver, muscle, and leukocytes, driving the ichthyosis and visceral disease.

evidence.reference_title: Growth retardation, impaired triacylglycerol catabolism, hepatic steatosis, and lethal skin barrier defect in mice lacking comparative gene identification-58 (CGI-58).
evidence.snippet:
Mutations in both ATGL and CGI-58 cause "neutral lipid storage disease" 
characterized by massive accumulation of TG in various tissues
